If our intention is to build an API database or dataset, we must do it in four stages, is described in four stages: data pre-processing, API database creation, instructions generation, and data validation.

 

1. Data Pre-processing stage involves filtering out files or malfunctioning endpoints, information about the API and its endpoints are extracted.

2. Create API DBs, an API database is created. This database contains instances in JSON format, each holding all relevant information about an endpoint, and the API it belongs to. API call examples should be included in each instance.

3. Instructions Generation by creating high-quality instruction examples and generating instruction candidates. The method involves selecting endpoints, bootstrapping information into templates, and refining the instructions, at this stage, the selected LLM is used to generate instructions based on the examples.

 

4. Data Validation stage involves verifying if the API calls are valid HTTP request examples, checking if the instructions are of high-quality, and selecting the instruction with the best quality for training. The LLM is used for various tasks in this stage, such as labeling instructions as good or bad, and calculating the likelihood of an LLM to recreate the input text used to generate each instruction.

Its clear that LLMs like GPT-4 require significant computational resources, making them inefficient and , for coding pourposes, there is a need for smaller, task-oriented LLMs that maintain functionality while reducing costs, however, smaller models have a higher risk of errors or "hallucinations" and issues with precise output formatting, which is critical for robust software applications, so the solution is headed for LLM Training and Inference, using a dataset of thousands of widely-used APIs from pourpose built datasets, covering diverse functionalities of the application we need to, with the goal  to improve LLMs' ability to select appropriate API functions. This might be done with small models like Codellama7b, Google's Gemma 7B & 2B, and Stable Code 3B.

The technical limitation lies on the 3rd step, if I try to create an OpenAPI spec for a single SAP API, I take for instance the Material Serial Number, service name: API_MATERIALSERIALNUMBER, to create, read and update serial numbers for a material, this API, in its most basic format for OpenAPI spec, will require the actual schemas for the respective entities and actions, in other words, we need to adjust it further than this for a single SAP API;

 

 

 

openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  version: '1.0.0'
  title: 'Material Serial Number API'
  description: 'API to create, read, update, and manage material serial numbers'
servers:
  - url: 'https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_MATERIALSERIALNUMBER_0001/resource'
paths:
  /MaterialSerialNumber:
    get:
      summary: 'Get entities from MaterialSerialNumber'
      description: 'Read data for all material serial numbers.'
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: Material
          schema:
            type: string
          description: 'Indicates the unique number that identifies a material.'
          required: true
        - in: query
          name: SerialNumber
          schema:
            type: string
          description: 'Indicates the number of the serialized equipment.'
          required: true
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
                type: object
    post:
      summary: 'Add new entity to MaterialSerialNumber'
      description: 'Create a material serial number.'
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
              type: object
      responses:
        '201':
          description: 'Created'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
                type: object
  /MaterialSerialNumber/SAP__self.CreateMassMaterialSerialNumber:
    post:
      summary: 'Invoke action CreateMassMaterialSerialNumber'
      description: 'Create Mass Material Serial Numbers'
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              # Add schema for CreateMassMaterialSerialNumber action
              type: object
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
                type: object
  /MaterialSerialNumber/{Material}/{SerialNumber}:
    get:
      summary: 'Get entity from MaterialSerialNumber by key'
      description: 'Read data for a particular material serial number.'
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: Material
          schema:
            type: string
          required: true
        - in: path
          name: SerialNumber
          schema:
            type: string
          required: true
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
                type: object
    patch:
      summary: 'Update entity in MaterialSerialNumber'
      description: 'Update StockInformation for an existing material serial number using PATCH request.'
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: Material
          schema:
            type: string
          required: true
        - in: path
          name: SerialNumber
          schema:
            type: string
          required: true
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
              type: object
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for MaterialSerialNumber entity
                type: object
  /MaterialSerialNumber/{Material}/{SerialNumber}/SAP__self.ChangeMaintenancePlant:
    post:
      summary: 'Invoke action ChangeMaintenancePlant'
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              # Add schema for ChangeMaintenancePlant action
              type: object
      responses:
        '204':
          description: 'No Content'
  # Add other paths and operations similarly
  /MaterialSerialNumber/{Material}/{SerialNumber}/_Partner:
    get:
      summary: 'Get entities from related _Partner'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for _Partner entity
                type: object
    post:
      summary: 'Add new entity to related _Partner'
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              # Add schema for _Partner entity
              type: object
      responses:
        '201':
          description: 'Created'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for _Partner entity
                type: object
  /MaterialSerialNumberPartner/{Material}/{SerialNumber}/{Equipment}/{PartnerFunction}/{EquipmentPartnerObjectNmbr}/_EquipmentMaterialSerialNumber:
    get:
      summary: 'Get related _EquipmentMaterialSerialNumber'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for _EquipmentMaterialSerialNumber entity
                type: object
  # Add other paths and operations similarly
  /$batch:
    post:
      summary: 'Send a group of requests'
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              # Add schema for batch request
              type: object
      responses:
        '200':
          description: 'OK'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                # Add schema for batch response
                type: object

 

 

Conclusion

I describe what Function Calling is and what capability brings in the models, and which models have been tested with Function Calling mechanisms. I have been trying to understand if any of the existing models have been trained with SAP Function Calling capabilities, and what it would require to fine tune a small model with SAP APIs, just in case you want to explore it, what it would take.

We discussed several options, Multi Modal, like Octopus, Tool Augmented LLMs, which is the most explored option and has been around for a while, Model Chaining and API Agents which is a very recent methodology as well as Tool Usage Planning which requires further investigation, all of them will require worth a lot of knowledge from the SAP team to make it work correctly, I also particularly started creating an SAP API Dataset and made it public on Hugging Face, which I will describe in a future blog.

Its almost June 2024 and we are entering what Gartner calls the "Trough of Disillusionment", although this methodology is not enough for SAP, keep the faith, don't loose trust on Generative AI, we'll get there.

entry.title: Get Ready for Sapphire 2024 with Three New SAP Business Technology Platform Use Cases
entry.content:
Can you believe it? It’s already time for SAP Sapphire 2024, happening in Orlando and Barcelona, as well as virtually. Join us for days of inspiration, innovation, and insight into the latest trends and technologies at SAP shaping the future of business.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform at SAPPHIRE

In this blog, I'll explore new use cases for 2024, focusing on SAP's key themes. We'll see how SAP Business Technology Platform, powered by Generative AI, can transform the value derived from other SAP business applications. These use cases come with their respective Discovery Center Missions and Business Accelerators, making your journey with SAP BTP both easy and efficient.

If you're heading to the in-person event in Orlando, make sure to catch our session, "Explore Business Scenarios Enabled by SAP BTP That You Can Implement Today." My colleague Denise Ma and I will be sharing practical tips and insights to help you jump-start your SAP BTP journey. It's a must-add to your agenda! Don't forget to stop by the SAP BTP: For Business Applications demo station, where our team will be ready to answer all your questions and help you get the most out of SAP BTP. Also, check out the following sessions by the team:

    • BTP108 | Transforming the future of Fresenius: RISE with SAP and SAP Analytics Cloud
    • BTP110 | Using cross-departmental data for analysis and planning with SAP S/4HANA
    • BTP111 | Driving the data and analytics strategy with SAP software at Lundin Mining
    • BTP154 | Enhance your investment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP BTP
    • BTP190 | How Nestle and Nokia accelerated transformation of SAP S/4HANA with SAP BTP

 

New Business Technology Platform Use Cases

In today's fast-moving business world, keeping up isn't enough; you need to be innovative, agile, and efficient. That's where SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) comes in. It helps you extend and personalize SAP applications, seamlessly integrate different systems, and empower your team with AI to streamline processes, improve experiences, and make confident decisions. Let's check out three exciting new SAP BTP use cases for 2024:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Use Case #1. Improve operations and decision-making with the power of Generative AI 

You: You're at a critical crossroads in deciding whether to prioritize modernizing your current landscape of business and processes or pivot towards AI-driven innovations. As per McKinsey’s 2023 survey*, Generative AI is already being regularly used at work among 22 percent of the survey respondents, and Generative AI is expected to significantly disrupt industry competition within three years. However, the risks and complexities involved in leveraging AI based on your business data are preventing you from making the jump. These include the challenges of integrating AI with third-party providers and existing systems, especially when the data involved is sensitive, raising concerns about accuracy and reliability.

How SAP BTP can help: Now is the ideal time to embrace AI, thoughtfully navigate these challenges, and realize the transformative potential it holds for your business. SAP BTP infuses business-ready, trusted, and user-friendly AI capabilities across the platform, to boost your business’ productivity and build AI solutions with your business-relevant and reliable data. Using AI in BTP can also boost the ROI of your existing SAP BTP investments, together with SAP Business applications and other third-party applications. Additionally, SAP BTP improves security by providing increased visibility and control over your AI usage with easily managed access to frontier LLMs, so your data is handled responsibly. Read more about SAP Business AI here.

Check out these sessions:

Orlando
  • BTP189 | Data in the Age of AI 
  • BTP192 | AI in SAP BTP – Vision and strategy
  • BTP197 | Take a lap through AI in SAP BTP
Barcelona
  • BTP210 | Data in the age of AI: Strategy and innovations
  • BTP218 | Unlocking AI's potential: The crucial role of data
  • BTP229 | ABAP Cloud model and generative AI: A power couple to keep your core clean
Virtual
  • PAR309v | Unlock the potential of your data assets: Data-driven decision-making
  • PAR310v | Transforming business with AI – Unlock value and performance

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Use Case #2. Empower employees with enterprise-wide self-service portals

You: Imagine your work monitor crowded with a patchwork of open applications, each window representing a different task you need to manage. Throughout the day, you find yourself clicking back and forth incessantly, switching between these various programs and interfaces. Your business relies on a mix of SAP and third-party solutions, each with its own unique user interface and experience. Harvard Business Review’s study in 2022** provides an example of a Fortune 500 consumer goods organization, where each person in a single supply-chain interaction has to switch about 350 times between 22 different applications and unique websites. Employees need to be trained on each of these solutions, which makes employee onboarding difficult. Many of them must log in to multiple systems to do their day-to-day work. The myriad of entry points to data across different lines of business only adds complexity to the mix.

How SAP BTP can help: SAP BTP answers the call with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. By consolidating tools and offering an intuitive and unified entry point for SAP and 3rd party applications, business processes, and information, SAP Build Work Zone fosters collaboration and efficiency. Role-based access paves the way for streamlined workflows, heightened productivity, and a culture of innovation. Giving employees a simplified user experience and access to multiple systems and processes, enabling them to build interactive workspaces visually to better engage with their teams and external stakeholders, truly unifies employee experience and increases productivity. Read more about SAP Build Work Zone here.

 

Orlando
  • BTP120 | Lockheed Martin: Fostering one employee experience with SAP Build Work Zone
  • BTP131 | What's new with SAP Build Work Zone and the SAP Build Apps tool
  • BTP186 | Transformation at Hatch Ltd. with SAP S/4HANA and SAP Build Work Zone
Barcelona
  • PAR240 | CEPSA ‘HR’evolution: Empowering people, transforming human resources
Virtual
  • HCM108v | Close the skills gap with total workforce management

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Use Case #3. Supercharge developer productivity with SAP Build Code and generative AI

You: You are an SAP developer, and many hours of your day are spent navigating complex application logic and checking data models. After all, IDC’s research*** states that developers spend 60% of their time on operational responsibilities, such as the implementation of automation, code testing and execution, while only 15% of their time is spent on coding. All those development and testing experiences while striving to shorten lead times to market pose significant hurdles for professional developers. Speed and flexibility while securing productivity are difficult to achieve.

How SAP BTP can help: SAP Build Code provides generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, empowering developers to overcome these challenges. It offers an intuitive, turn-key design time and runtime environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. Use AI to generate app logic, data models, and test scripts, and experience how SAP Build Code accelerates time-to-value for development projects and supercharges developer productivity. Read more about SAP Build Code here.

 

Orlando
  • BTP117 | Generative AI–based code development from SAP Build Code
  • BTP118 | Discover generative AI-based code development from SAP Build Code
  • BTP119 | Application development with SAP Build and SAP Build Code: Road map review
Barcelona
  • BTP138 | Generative AI–based code development from SAP Build Code
Virtual
  • BTP224v | Extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build and SAP Build Code

 

Honourable Mention:

Accelerate Financial Planning & Analysis

You: Working and collaborating across the organization gets overwhelming, as Financial Planning & Analysis is not a one-person or even one-department job; the CEO, the offices of CFO, and the other stakeholders across the business all hold key business data and processes crucial to FP&A, but it seems like each area has their own rules and data in different places. This causes delays in accessing the real-time data, impeding proactive planning and agile decision-making.

How SAP BTP can help: With the power of data integration, visualization, and predictive analytics, SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning serves as a reporting, analytics, and planning tool in one. It allows business users to access current real-time data in a simple and timely manner, eliminating delays in the planning process. Your financial planning projects can be fast-tracked with SAP’s prebuilt business accelerators, and activating predictive analytics with Machine Learning capabilities is as easy as a single click. Read more on SAP’s Financial Planning and Analysis solutions here.

 

To wrap up

SAP Business Technology Platform provides organizations with the tools and capabilities to thrive in the digital age. Through the lens of Generative AI, self-service portals, and developer productivity tools, SAP BTP not only addresses challenges but also unlocks opportunities for growth and innovation. It's not just about staying ahead; it's about leading the charge towards excellence. Now that these three new use cases have piqued your interest, visit SAP Business Technology Platform In Action community page for the full list of SAP BTP Use Cases and other resources. 

With a lineup of industry experts, hands-on workshops, and networking opportunities, Sapphire 2024 is not to be missed. We look forward to seeing you in Orlando, Barcelona, or virtually and sharing more about what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for you and your organization. Get ready to unlock your business's full potential and explore the possibilities of the intelligent enterprise.

 

Additional Resources

  • HCM 102 | Technology strategy and road map for SAP SuccessFactors solutions session to learn more about how SAP BTP works with SAP SuccessFactors.

 

*The state of AI in 2023: Generative AI’s breakout year. (2023, August 1). McKinsey & Company. https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai-in-2023-generative-ais-breakout-year

**Murty, R. N. (2022, November 1). How much time and energy do we waste toggling between applications? Harvard Business Review. https://hbr.org/2022/08/how-much-time-and-energy-do-we-waste-toggling-between-applications

***IDC: Generative AI and turnkey development platforms can improve developer productivity. (n.d.). SAP. https://www.sap.com/canada/documents/2024/01/fe86d503-a57e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

entry.title: Unlocking AI Potential with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
entry.content:
As SAP Sapphire 2024 approaches, we invite you to explore the exciting world of AI within SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Whether you are attending in person at Orlando or Barcelona, or joining us virtually, these resources will empower you to infuse AI into your applications and drive innovation

BTP Interactive learning tools: Get guidance with available SAP resources covering aspects like application development, integration, data analytics, and AI

  1. Code Samples: Generative AI hub starter kit 
  1. Reference architecture: Optimal setup guides

Knowledge Building Tools: Collaborate and learn how to use Business AI to implement use cases with certification programs and hands-on developer tutorials

  1. Certification Programs: Recognized learning paths for Business AI 
  1. Developer Videos: Hands-on videos on BTP use cases with AI 
  1. Community: Be part of our new public group for AI enthusiasts 

Expert Assistance: Find resources to support your BTP AI journey

  1. SAP Certified Partners: Find SAP Partners 
  1. Tailored SAP Support: SAP Support Portal

entry.title: AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework
entry.content:
In the first part of the AI@FRE Blog Series, we discussed the challenges of Enterprise AI adoption, what are AI embedding solutions, and quick-start use-cases for integrating AI capabilities. We also highlighted how the Future Ready Enterprise (FRE) Engagement Model supports customers in their digital transformation journeys, facilitating the onboarding and maturation of AI technologies.

 

In this second part, we will delve into:

  • FRE Values Lens for Technology Platform Domain Adoption and Expansion
  • The benefits of choosing FRE for AI adoption, including expected architecture and business values derived from the FRE Engagement Model

 

FRE: A Structured Value Framework for SAP Business AI Adoption

The Future Ready Enterprise (#FRE) framework provides BTP customers and partners a clear structure for advancing their domain maturity: how to start, how to scale, and how to achieve platform excellence.

This engagement model aims to accelerate customers' ability to Operate at Pace, Protect Core systems, and Embrace technological and business changes.

A digital enterprise needs a robust innovation platform to drive its digital transformation journey, ensuring it can effectively leverage emerging technologies like AI to remain competitive and agile.

FRE Value Framework

The Value Framework of FRE is designed to broaden the customer's perspective on the problems that can be solved with BTP. Our goal is to shift the paradigm that BTP is primarily a bolt-on integration. This framework offers a way to better understand the flexibility of FRE and the value that BTP can unlock.

It includes several Value Lenses to customers / partners:

1.  Business Process Excellence:

This lens ensures we apply a business perspective rather than just seeing technology as a tool. It encourages us to view the customer's organization from an outside-in approach driven by business processes. This helps prioritize technology solutions that deliver the highest value. Key focus areas include the design and modeling of processes, encompassing automation, extension, optimization, acceleration, and insight to action.

As businesses grow and innovate, they must optimize processes spanning different LoBs and systems, to identify inefficiencies and ensure compliance with legal obligations. For an in-depth look at how SAP’s Intelligent Business Process Management on BTP helps organizations achieve process excellence through Process Discovery, Process Automation & Integration, and Process Monitoring, enabling digital and automated end-to-end visibility and integration of business processes, click to the related blog article

2.  Scalable Architecture:

This lens is essential for cloud transitions, focusing on creating a cloud environment that automatically adjusts resources to handle increased workloads without compromising performance.

Key focus areas include:

  • Harmonize & Optimize: Aligning and refining components, processes, and resources for seamless integration and efficient utilization.
  • Re-platform: Migrating or upgrading applications to a cloud platform, adjusting to leverage new platform benefits.
  • Protect the Core: Minimizing complex customizations in core systems and utilizing platform capabilities for innovation and change.

To learn more about achieving scalable architecture with SAP BTP, visit this blog's content on best practices and tools for building scalable and secure applications.

 

3.  Architecture Readiness

This lens assesses the readiness of an organization's technology infrastructure, applications, and processes for transitioning to a cloud-based environment. It involves evaluating the current architecture, identifying necessary modifications, and ensuring alignment with cloud platform requirements.

BTP technology domains include: Intelligent Technologies, Application Architecture, UX/Mobile, Integration, Hyper Automation, Data, Analytics, Security

 

4.  Operate at Pace

This lens focuses on the ability to execute cloud architecture effectively.

Key focus areas include:

  • Cloud Native Development: Leveraging cloud computing, microservices, containerization, DevOps, CI/CD, elasticity, resilience, API and event-first principles, statelessness, and infrastructure as code.
  • Low Code and No Code: Utilizing platforms that enable rapid application creation through pre-built components (low-code) or visual interfaces (no-code) without writing code.
  • Customization and Extension: Tailoring and extending application features with new modules, plugins, and integrations.

Read about the "Operate at Pace" story from Hitachi High-Tech on their successful implementation of a 2-Tier S/4HANA Cloud with Clean Core using SAP BTP, significantly reducing add-ons from over 9,000 to fewer than 600.

 

 5.  Reimagine Business Process

This lens involves fundamentally rethinking and redesigning traditional business processes using the platform as an innovation tool. It aims to make processes more efficient, agile, customer-centric, and aligned with strategic goals. This lens challenges conventional norms and seeks innovative ways to create value, optimize operations, and gain a competitive edge.

This approach helps shift the perception of BTP from being merely a gateway to SAP to a pathway for sustainable innovation and competitive differentiation.

Discover how to Future-Proof your business and "Reimagine Business Processes" with SAP BTP, featuring innovations across entire application landscape: in integration, process insights, low-code automation, and a new open data offering in partnership with Google.

 

As previously mentioned, the FRE Framework encapsulates the capabilities, opportunities, domains, and outcomes achievable through leveraging SAP BTP in your landscape. It also forms the foundation of the FRE Engagement Approach, aimed at helping customers become future-ready and realize value by increasing the maturity of relevant BTP domains.

 

What Are the Expected Values from the FRE Engagement Model in AI Adoption Journey?

The FRE Engagement Model offers significant value through two primary lenses: Architecture Values and Business Values.

Architecture Values: Through the architecture values lens, we emphasize the importance of standardization in data, UX, and security. This standardization is expected to provide a robust foundation for scalable and efficient AI adoption.

Business Values: Here we focus on business process excellence, encouraging organizations to reimagine processes for greater efficiency. This approach ensures continuous improvement and sustainable growth.

FRE Value Framework for Business AI Adoption

1.  Business Process Excellence:

  • Definition: Leveraging SAP Business AI to accelerate, automate, and optimize business processes.
  • Expected Values: Empower your organization by streamlining operations, enhancing collaboration, and driving growth.

Discover how FC Bayern achieves "Business Process Excellence" in SuccessFactors by embedding SAP AI capabilities in talent management and recruitment refinement, to enhance HR functions and processes.

 

2.  Scalable Architecture:

  • Definition: Utilizing SAP Business AI to protect core systems and prepare your landscape for the future.
  • Expected Values: Modernize your landscape by embracing agility, efficiency, and cost-effectiveness.

 

3.  Architecture Readiness:

  • Definition: Simplifying architecture across the enterprise to reduce costs and prepare the organization for the future.
  • Expected Values: Achieve strategic alignment by simplifying solutions, enhancing efficiency, and strengthening security.

Discover how Heartland Dental attains UX standardization with Build Work Zone, preparing them to embed AI innovation and enhance efficiency, job satisfaction, and patient experience.

 

4.  Operate at Pace:

  • Definition: Using SAP BTP to develop generative AI-powered business applications and extensions in a cloud-native environment.
  • Expected Values: Enable agile business processes with simplified upgrade cycles, end-to-end transparency, and the ability to embrace change.

 

Discover how Foodstuffs South Island prepares for future AI adoption with SAP S/4 HANA RISE, driving operational efficiencies, transforming member and supplier experiences, and meeting customer demand for fresh and affordable groceries; while also planning to leverage SAP’s embedded AI capabilities within SAP BTP Core for improved forecasting and inventory management.

 

5.  Reimagine Business Processes:

  • Definition: Empowering new business models, facilitating data-driven decision-making, and preparing your workforce for the future with high-quality business data supported by state-of-the-art SAP Business AI functionality.
  • Expected Values: Foster innovation by embracing agility, creating a composable enterprise, and enhancing strategic planning.

Discover how Komplett Group ASA, a pioneering electronic retailer in Norway, reimagines its business processes with SAP Integration Suite, enabling real-time event-driven architecture and ensuring responsiveness to customer needs, while reshaping its IT landscape to keep up with market changes.

By focusing on these dimensions, the FRE Engagement Model provides a comprehensive approach to maximizing the benefits of AI adoption in your digital transformation journey.

 

For more information on BTP FRE Engagement Model and how FRE can establish foundational capabilities for customer's AI Adoption, please feel free to reach out to our team at BTPFRE@sap.com

entry.title: Save the Date for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we offer a fully-fledged cloud ERP that optimizes processes and helps your business to run smoothly. We as the Cloud ERP PSCC organization strive to enable our valued customers and partners to take full advantage of the benefits our solution brings for the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

Therefore, we offer a variety of enablement assets to explain the features and functions of each new release and make sure that you get the maximum value out of your investment.

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a series of compelling live product expert sessions that takes place twice a year and is designed by product experts for product experts to give customers, partners, and colleagues a special insider’s preview into the highlights for selected lines of business and industries, 1-2 weeks before the actual release. In a span of multiple days, we give you the opportunity to see the latest demos, ask your questions, and get answers straight from the product experts before everyone else.

In July 2024, we will be back with a set of compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

🗓️ Don't miss this chance and save the date!

Stay tuned for the complete agenda and registration links to follow.

Find the recordings of previous live sessions here, here, and here.

How to Sign Up for the SAP Enterprise Support Edition as a Customer/Partner

In order to sign up for the online sessions, you will need to have an active SAP Learning Hub license. While the series is available across all editions, you can find the steps below how to register for the SAP Enterprise Support edition in case you don’t already have access to any of the editions yet.

  • Navigate to the sign up page 
  • Click the Sign up button. Authenticate yourself with your s-user ID and password. Upon first access, the system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalog respectively. Within two hours, you will then receive a registration confirmation via e-mail.
  • After you have received the confirmation e-mail, the registration process is complete.

 

How to Sign Up for the Internal Edition as an SAP Employee

In order to sign up for the online sessions, you will need to have an SAP Learning Hub license. In case you have no edition assigned to your profile, please register here for the Internal Edition which is free of charge.

Make sure you be logged onto the SAP Learning Hub before opening the URL. In case the link doesn't open, copy and paste it into your browser. If you face any other issues with your login, please contact the help center.

Follow the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tag and the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with the latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @Sap and #S4HANA, and myself on here, LinkedIn, and X.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out. 

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

 

🎯Where to Find More Information:

  • Read up on the highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/highlights-of-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-release/ba-p/13587480
  • Interested in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 update on the intelligent ERP? Read all about it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-intelligent-erp-update/ba-p/13584760 
  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-exclusive-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402/ba-p/13587019
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Watch our SAP Expert Talks here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDl0m4Wlj7ttWqA1on5oxwvc 
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Find out how to switch from groups to spaces and pages here: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/11/06/deactivation-of-groups-in-the-sap-fiori-launchpad-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-switching-to-spaces-and-pages-mandatory/
  • Listen to our podcast here: https://podcast.opensap.info/inside-sap/
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace here: https://microlearning.opensap.com/
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest?region=DE
  • Check out what’s new here: https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226  
  • Find help here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD
  • Read the feature scope description here: https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3
